title: peucedanum caruifolium
intro: Peucedanum caruifolium, also known as Caraway-leaved Masterwort, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. It has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as to treat digestive problems and menstrual cramps. Peucedanum caruifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile natural remedy for various health concerns.
